mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate held above 5% for multiple months raises the equity discount rate and increases the opportunity cost of holding stocks relative to short-term bills, producing a persistent negative drag on S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no significant negative bias (t-test p > 0.05) when FEDFUNDS is above 5% across a 3-year rolling sample, or Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over three months, it signals an inflection into labor market deterioration that historically precedes recessionary equity drawdowns, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: Following the 0.3pp UNRATE rise trigger, 60-day forward SPX returns are positive in more than 55% of historical trigger instances, invalidating the recessionary signal.

mechanism_narrative: A high real policy rate, measured as FEDFUNDS minus year-over-year CPIAUCSL inflation, compresses valuation multiples only when both components move jointly against risk assets; the combined tightening signal predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days beyond what either series alone implies.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS minus CPIAUCSL YoY inflation exceeds 2pp
failure_condition: The joint real-rate signal fails to outperform the single-series FEDFUNDS-only model in out-of-sample Rank IC, or produces IC below 0.03 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) declining sharply during a monetary tightening regime reflect forward-looking demand destruction from higher mortgage rates, which propagates through consumer wealth and construction employment channels to depress S&P 500 returns over the next quarter, but this relationship is muted or reversed outside tightening regimes.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls > 10% YoY while FEDFUNDS above 4%
failure_condition: During periods with FEDFUNDS above 4% and HOUST declines exceeding 10% YoY, 60-day forward SPX returns are not significantly negative (p > 0.05), or the effect reverses sign in an easing regime test.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects expanding real output and corporate earnings capacity, generating a short-horizon positive drift in S&P 500 prices as analysts revise earnings expectations upward over the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward SPX returns show no positive correlation with trailing INDPRO growth (Rank IC < 0.03) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A deceleration in monthly PAYEMS growth below 100,000 net jobs added signals weakening labor demand that precedes reduced consumer spending and corporate revenue growth, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month once the threshold is breached.
candidate_trigger: monthly PAYEMS net change falls below 100k
failure_condition: When PAYEMS net monthly change falls below 100k, 20-day forward SPX returns are positive in more than 55% of instances, refuting the deceleration-driven weakness hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: Real retail sales growth, approximated by RSAFS growth net of CPIAUCSL inflation, captures genuine consumer demand momentum; when both series jointly indicate accelerating real spending, S&P 500 returns rise over the following week as consumer discretionary earnings expectations improve.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS YoY growth exceeds CPIAUCSL YoY inflation by 2pp
failure_condition: The joint real-retail-sales signal fails to produce Rank IC above 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or underperforms an RSAFS-only baseline model.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion regime where GDPC1 quarterly growth decelerates below its trailing four-quarter average, equity markets price in an elevated probability of a growth slowdown, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter; this relationship does not hold during early-cycle recoveries where deceleration reflects normalization rather than exhaustion.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 QoQ annualized growth falls below trailing 4-quarter average
failure_condition: 60-day forward SPX returns following a GDPC1 deceleration signal are not significantly negative (p > 0.05) when restricted to late-cycle expansion periods, or the sign flips in early-cycle recovery subsample tests.
